fire_alarm_system_types.principle: The building is divided into zones. Each zone contains multiple detectors connected to the control panel.
disadvantages: Cannot identify the exact detector location

fire_alarm_system_types.principle: Each device has a unique digital address and communicates with the control panel through a loop.

types.description: Central unit controlling all fire alarm devices, monitors zones and initiates alarms.
types.working_principle: Receives signals from detectors and manual devices, processes them, and triggers notifications.
types.installation: Installed in a secure control room, easily accessible for fire safety personnel.

types.description: Controls basic fire alarm systems using conventional circuits.
types.working_principle: Monitors individual circuits and triggers alarms on detection.
types.installation: Installed in control rooms or accessible panels in buildings.

types.description: Remote panel displays status of FACP with LEDs and indicators.
types.working_principle: Connects to FACP to mirror alarms and system status.
types.installation: Installed at building entrances or security rooms for quick access.

types.description: Repeats the main FACP status in different locations within a building.
types.working_principle: Mirrors alarms, faults, and system indicators from the main panel.
types.installation: Installed at strategic locations to increase visibility of alarms.

types.description: Manages multiple FACP systems over a network for large facilities.
types.working_principle: Connects to multiple panels via IP network and centralizes control.
types.installation: Installed in central control rooms or data centers.

types.description: Provides regulated power to fire alarm panels and devices.
types.working_principle: Converts AC mains to DC for panels and ensures battery charging.
types.installation: Installed near FACP with proper ventilation.

types.description: Provides emergency backup power to FACP during mains failure.
types.working_principle: Stores electrical energy to maintain panel operation during power outage.
types.installation: Installed near FACP with ventilation and secure housing.

types.description: Detects smoke particles from slow-burning fires and thick smoke.
types.working_principle: Uses light scattering to detect smoke.
types.installation: Installed on ceilings away from walls.

types.description: Detects smoke from fast-burning fires and flames.
types.working_principle: Uses ionization chamber to detect smoke particles.
types.installation: Installed on ceilings; avoid kitchens and dusty areas.

types.description: Combines Photoelectric and Ionization sensors for maximum detection.
types.working_principle: Two detection methods in one unit.
types.installation: Installed on ceilings in critical areas.

types.description: Detects fire when the temperature exceeds a preset limit.
types.working_principle: Triggers alarm when ambient temperature reaches threshold.
types.installation: Installed in kitchens, boiler rooms, garages; away from direct sunlight.

types.description: Detects rapid increase in temperature, regardless of absolute value.
types.working_principle: Triggers alarm when temperature rises faster than a set rate.
types.installation: Ideal for areas where temperature fluctuates; away from heaters.

types.description: Combines Fixed Temperature and Rate-of-Rise detection for enhanced safety.
types.working_principle: Detects either high temperature or rapid temperature increase.
types.installation: Installed in critical areas with variable temperatures.

types.description: Detects flames using ultraviolet (UV) radiation.
types.working_principle: Senses UV light emitted by flames.
types.installation: Installed where open flames may occur; avoid direct sunlight.

types.description: Detects flames using infrared (IR) radiation.
types.working_principle: Senses IR radiation emitted by hot gases and flames.
types.installation: Installed in areas prone to hydrocarbon fires; avoid reflective surfaces.

types.description: Combines UV and IR sensors for maximum flame detection.
types.working_principle: Detects both UV and IR radiation to reduce false alarms.
types.installation: Installed in high-risk industrial areas with potential flames.

types.description: Detects toxic gases like CO, H2S using electrochemical sensors.
types.working_principle: Chemical reaction generates electrical current proportional to gas concentration.
types.installation: Installed near breathing zones, away from walls and corners.

types.description: Detects flammable gases like methane or propane using IR absorption.
types.working_principle: Measures absorption of IR light by gas molecules.
types.installation: Installed at potential leak points or ceiling for lighter-than-air gases.

types.description: Detects flammable gases by oxidizing them on a catalytic sensor.
types.working_principle: Combustion reaction increases sensor temperature, generating a measurable signal.
types.installation: Installed near potential gas leak sources, in well-ventilated areas.

types.description: Activates the fire alarm by pressing once; simple and fast to use.
types.working_principle: Mechanical switch triggers the fire alarm panel immediately.
types.installation: Installed at exit routes and corridors at 1.4m height from the floor.

types.description: Requires lifting cover then pressing to activate the alarm, preventing false activation.
types.working_principle: Mechanical switch with cover mechanism triggers alarm panel.
types.installation: Installed at strategic points in public areas for added safety.

types.description: Activates alarm by breaking the glass; commonly used in older buildings.
types.working_principle: Glass break closes a mechanical circuit to trigger the alarm.
types.installation: Installed at exit routes, corridors, with protective glass cover.

types.description: Pull lever down once to activate the fire alarm; fast and simple.
types.working_principle: Mechanical lever closes circuit to trigger fire alarm panel.
types.installation: Installed near exits and stairwells at 1.4m height.

types.description: Traditional bell produces loud ring to alert occupants.
types.working_principle: Electromagnet strikes bell creating audible alert.
types.installation: Installed in central corridors for maximum coverage.

types.description: Produces loud, adjustable frequency sound for fire alarm alerts.
types.working_principle: Electronic oscillator drives piezo speaker to generate sound.
types.installation: Installed in hallways, corridors, and rooms with high occupancy.

types.description: Loud siren to alert large buildings or industrial areas.
types.working_principle: Electromechanical or electronic circuit generates piercing sound.
types.installation: Installed in industrial zones, rooftops, or large halls.

types.description: Produces loud audible sound to alert occupants of fire.
types.working_principle: Electronic oscillator drives a piezo speaker to produce high decibel sound.
types.installation: Installed in corridors, rooms, and hallways for maximum coverage.

types.description: Combines audible horn and visual strobe for alerting in noisy areas.
types.working_principle: Horn produces sound; LED strobe flashes simultaneously.
types.installation: Installed in hallways, industrial zones, or areas with high ambient noise.

types.description: Visual alert using high-intensity LED flashes.
types.working_principle: Electronic circuit flashes LED at a set frequency to alert visually.
types.installation: Installed in corridors, rooms, and areas with high noise levels.

types.description: Provides voice instructions during fire emergencies.
types.working_principle: Receives messages from fire alarm panel and outputs voice audio.
types.installation: Installed in corridors, rooms, stairwells, and public areas.

types.description: Receives signals from external devices and forwards them to the fire alarm control panel.
types.working_principle: Monitors contact closures or voltage changes from connected devices.
types.installation: Installed near the devices it monitors, in secure enclosures.

types.description: Controls external devices such as fans, doors, or alarms based on fire alarm events.
types.working_principle: Activates relays when a condition is triggered by the control panel.
types.installation: Installed near devices it controls, in accessible enclosures.

types.description: Monitors the status of external devices and reports faults or alarms to the control panel.
types.working_principle: Continuously checks line integrity and device status.
types.installation: Installed near devices to be monitored.

types.description: Allows the fire alarm control panel to remotely control external devices.
types.working_principle: Receives commands from the panel and switches connected devices accordingly.
types.installation: Installed in secure enclosures near controlled devices.

types.description: Provides isolated relay outputs for triggering external systems.
types.working_principle: Relay closes/open contacts when activated by the fire alarm panel.
types.installation: Installed close to devices it controls in secure enclosures.

types.description: Connects addressable devices to the control panel for communication and monitoring.
types.working_principle: Assigns unique addresses to devices for individual monitoring and control.
types.installation: Installed in zones with addressable devices, near the panel or distribution points.

types.description: Transmits fire alarm signals to a central monitoring station or remote site.
types.working_principle: Sends digital or analog signals over dedicated communication lines.
types.installation: Installed near the control panel or communication hub.

types.description: Sends fire alarm notifications via cellular network (SMS or data) to monitoring stations or users.
types.working_principle: Uses GSM network to relay alarm events.
types.installation: Installed near control panel with cellular signal coverage.

types.description: Sends fire alarm notifications over TCP/IP networks to monitoring systems or software.
types.working_principle: Encodes alarm data and transmits via Ethernet.
types.installation: Installed near control panel with network connection.

types.description: Automatically calls pre-programmed phone numbers to report fire alarm events.
types.working_principle: Connects to telephone line and sends voice/data signals when alarm triggers.
types.installation: Installed near the control panel with telephone line access.

types.description: Protects sections of the fire alarm loop by isolating faults to prevent total system failure.
types.working_principle: Automatically disconnects faulty section while maintaining operation of the rest of the loop.
types.installation: Installed on the loop wiring at strategic points to isolate sections.

types.description: Protects the fire alarm loop by isolating short circuit faults.
types.working_principle: Automatically disconnects the shorted section while keeping other sections active.
types.installation: Installed along loop wiring where short circuit risk is high.

types.description: Allows connection of devices to the addressable fire alarm loop.
types.working_principle: Assigns unique addresses to devices for monitoring and control.
types.installation: Installed along the addressable loop near device groups.

types.description: Monitors individual zones for faults or alarms and reports to the fire alarm panel.
types.working_principle: Supervises circuits and provides status signals to the panel.
types.installation: Installed in control panel or near monitored zones.

types.description: Fire-resistant cable that maintains circuit integrity during a fire.
types.working_principle: Special insulation prevents short circuits and maintains signal transmission under high temperature.
types.installation: Used for connecting detectors, control panels, and notification devices; routed through conduits or trays.

types.description: Protects wire connections and provides access for maintenance.
types.working_principle: Encloses and organizes wire splices and terminals.
types.installation: Installed at wiring junction points along loops; mounted on walls or ceilings.

types.description: Protects fire alarm wiring and provides pathway for cables.
types.working_principle: Encloses cables to prevent physical damage and fire propagation.
types.installation: Installed along walls or ceilings; connectors at junctions.

types.description: Supports and organizes multiple fire alarm cables along a path.
types.working_principle: Maintains proper cable routing and prevents stress or entanglement.
types.installation: Mounted on ceilings or walls; used in commercial/industrial buildings.

types.description: Terminates the end of a circuit for supervision by the fire alarm panel.
types.working_principle: Maintains a constant resistance for panel to detect open/short circuits.
types.installation: Connected at the last device in the loop or zone wiring.

types.description: Draws air through a network of pipes to detect smoke at very early stages.
types.working_principle: Air samples are analyzed by a central detection unit for smoke particles.
types.installation: Pipe network installed across the protected area; central detection unit in control room.

types.description: Uses a light beam across large open spaces to detect smoke.
types.working_principle: Smoke obstructs the beam, triggering an alarm.
types.installation: Ceiling-mounted; transmitter and receiver aligned across open area.

types.description: Detects smoke in HVAC ducts to prevent smoke spread.
types.working_principle: Airflow through detector chamber is monitored for smoke particles.
types.installation: Installed inside ductwork with sampling tube or probe.

types.description: Continuously samples air to detect smoke particles at early stages.
types.working_principle: Air is drawn through a detection unit with laser or photoelectric sensors.
types.installation: Pipe network installed in protected spaces; detection unit centrally located.

types.description: Integrates the fire alarm system with elevator control to ensure safe operation during fire.
types.working_principle: Sends signals to recall elevators to a designated floor and disables operation if fire is detected.
types.installation: Mounted near elevator control panel or fire alarm control panel.

types.description: Integrates fire alarm with HVAC system to stop smoke propagation.
types.working_principle: Signals HVAC system to shut down fans and dampers when fire is detected.
types.installation: Installed near HVAC control panel or FA system panel.

types.description: Controls automatic release of fire doors in case of emergency.
types.working_principle: Receives signal from FA panel to unlock fire doors or hold-open devices.
types.installation: Mounted near fire doors or FA panel; connected to door mechanism.

types.description: Connects fire alarm system with fire pump to ensure water supply activation.
types.working_principle: Sends start/stop signal to pump controller upon alarm or test.
types.installation: Mounted near pump controller or FA control panel.

types.description: Monitors sprinkler water flow and integrates with fire alarm system.
types.working_principle: Detects water movement in sprinkler piping and signals FA panel.
types.installation: Installed on sprinkler riser or branch line near control valve.

types.description: Detects water movement in sprinkler piping to monitor activation.
types.working_principle: Mechanical paddle or sensor detects flow and signals the fire alarm panel.
types.installation: Installed on sprinkler riser or main branch line near control valve.

types.description: Monitors pressure in sprinkler or fire suppression systems.
types.working_principle: Switch activates when system pressure falls below or rises above set limits.
types.installation: Mounted on piping or pump discharge; near FA control panel.

types.description: Detects unauthorized access or tampering with fire system valves.
types.working_principle: Mechanical switch triggers alarm when valve cover or handle is moved.
types.installation: Mounted on control valves, sprinkler risers, or cabinets.

types.description: Monitors status of supervisory devices like valve positions or system integrity.
types.working_principle: Triggers supervisory alarm when abnormal condition is detected.
types.installation: Installed on valves, panels, or system components as required.

fire_fighting_system_types.principle: A network of pipes filled with water connected to sprinkler heads that automatically discharge water when the temperature reaches a specific threshold.
characteristics: Uses water as the extinguishing agent
disadvantages: Not suitable for electrical equipment rooms

fire_fighting_system_types.principle: A system that provides firefighters with a reliable water supply through hydrants, hoses, and valves to manually fight fires.
characteristics: Connected to a high-capacity water supply
Used by trained personnel or firefighters

fire_fighting_system_types.principle: Uses foam solution to create a blanket over flammable liquids, preventing oxygen from reaching the fuel and suppressing the fire.
characteristics: Uses foam concentrate mixed with water

fire_fighting_system_types.principle: Uses clean agent gases to suppress fire by reducing oxygen concentration or interrupting the chemical reaction of combustion.
Discharges very fine water droplets that absorb heat rapidly and reduce oxygen around the fire.
characteristics: Consumes less water than traditional sprinklers

types.description: Produces loud audible alarm to alert occupants of fire.
types.working_principle: Mechanical or electronic bell triggered by fire alarm panel.
types.installation: Installed at strategic locations throughout the building.

types.description: Produces continuous or pulsed loud sound to alert occupants.
types.working_principle: Electronic sounder activated by fire alarm control panel.
types.installation: Installed in corridors, halls, and high-occupancy areas.

types.description: Provides visual alert to occupants during fire alarm activation.
types.working_principle: Flashing LED light triggered by fire alarm panel.
types.installation: Installed in corridors, restrooms, and areas where audible alarms may not be heard.

types.description: Delivers pre-recorded or live voice messages for evacuation.
types.working_principle: Activated by fire alarm panel to broadcast instructions via speakers.
types.installation: Installed throughout building in halls, corridors, and large rooms.

types.description: Automatically sprays water to suppress fire when temperature exceeds threshold.
types.working_principle: Thermal element triggers water flow through piping network.
types.installation: Installed on ceilings covering designated hazard areas.

types.description: Provides water for manual firefighting; connected to building water supply.
types.working_principle: Open valve to release water through hose onto fire.
types.installation: Installed in cabinets near exits and stairwells.

types.description: Provides external water source for firefighting operations.
types.working_principle: Connect fire hoses to hydrant outlets to access water.
types.installation: Installed outdoors at strategic locations accessible to fire services.

types.description: Maintains system pressure and provides water to sprinklers and hydrants.
types.working_principle: Automatically starts when pressure drops; powered electrically or by diesel.
types.installation: Installed in pump rooms connected to water supply and fire protection system.

types.description: Stores water dedicated for fire suppression systems.
types.working_principle: Provides constant water supply to fire pumps and sprinklers.
types.installation: Installed aboveground or underground near pump rooms.

types.description: Extinguishes flammable liquid fires using foam blanket.
types.working_principle: Mixes foam concentrate with water and disperses over fire.
types.installation: Installed in areas with high risk of flammable liquid fires.

types.description: Extinguishes fire by displacing oxygen in the hazard area.
types.working_principle: CO₂ released from cylinders to flood the protected area.
types.installation: Installed in high-risk areas such as server rooms and electrical panels.

types.description: Extinguishes Class A, B, and C fires using dry chemical powder.
types.working_principle: Powder discharged under pressure to suppress fire.
types.installation: Installed in areas with high fire risk such as industrial kitchens and chemical storage.

types.description: Central panel controlling all fire fighting equipment and monitoring system status.
types.working_principle: Monitors pumps, sprinklers, foam/CO₂ systems, receives signals from detectors, and integrates with building systems.
types.installation: Installed in a secure pump/control room with easy access for fire safety personnel.
features: Monitoring and operation of pumps (Jockey, Main, Diesel)
Control of sprinklers, foam, and CO₂ systems
Receiving signals from smoke, heat, and flow detectors
Integration with building systems (Elevator, HVAC, Fire Doors)

types.description: Interfaces fire fighting system with building elevators to control recall and prevent use during fire.
types.working_principle: Sends signals from fire panel to elevator control to initiate recall or lock functions.
types.installation: Installed near elevator control panels or main FFCP.

types.description: Automatically shuts down HVAC systems to prevent smoke spread during fire.
types.working_principle: Receives signals from FFCP and deactivates HVAC fans and dampers.
types.installation: Installed near HVAC control panel or FFCP.

types.description: Controls automatic fire doors, releasing them in case of fire or alarm activation.
types.working_principle: Receives FFCP signals to unlock or close fire doors as needed.
types.installation: Installed near door frames or integrated with door actuator.

types.description: Monitors water flow in sprinkler systems and signals FFCP for alarms.
types.working_principle: Detects water movement and closes/open contacts to notify control panel.
types.installation: Installed in sprinkler risers or main water lines.

types.description: Controls fire pumps automatically based on system demand or alarm activation.
types.working_principle: Monitors pressure switches and signals pumps to start/stop for firefighting.
types.installation: Installed in pump room near FFCP or main pump system.

types.description: Monitors water flow in sprinkler systems and notifies the FFCP in case of activation.
types.working_principle: Detects movement of water in pipes and closes/open contacts to send signal to control panel.
types.installation: Installed in sprinkler risers or main water lines.

types.description: Monitors pressure in fire fighting systems and signals FFCP for pump activation or alarms.
types.working_principle: Senses pressure changes and closes/open contacts to trigger system response.
types.installation: Installed in main pump lines or risers.

types.description: Detects unauthorized tampering or opening of fire fighting equipment enclosures.
types.working_principle: Switch triggers when enclosure or valve is opened, sending signal to FFCP.
types.installation: Installed on valve covers, cabinets, or equipment housings.

types.description: Monitors the status of fire fighting system components and reports abnormal conditions.
types.working_principle: Switch closes or opens contacts to notify FFCP of supervisory conditions (e.g., valve closed, system inactive).
types.installation: Installed on valves, controllers, or supervisory points.
